beats.activity: Wake up, pour-over coffee, review work schedule and weather.
beats.reasoning: Morning ritual; Kwame starts with deliberate coffee prep and mental planning. Rain forecast means he'll need to think about commute routing around SR-520 closure.

beats.activity: Drive to Redmond via I-90 east, avoiding SR-520 eastbound closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 is closed 9am–noon eastbound, so Kwame leaves early to use I-90 alternate route; steady rain from 7am onward makes him cautious about timing. Extra buffer absorbed in commute window.

beats.activity: Bike mechanic work: repair flats, adjust derailleurs, inspect customer bikes for rain damage.
beats.reasoning: Rainy morning means more drivetrain corrosion complaints and flat repairs; Kwame settles into steady mechanical work. Mentoring Tyler on weather-related maintenance checks.

beats.activity: Lunch at the café near the campus; grab a sandwich and espresso.
beats.reasoning: Rain still falling; Kwame stays indoors and nearby rather than venturing far. Espresso over drip coffee, as always. Solid routine lunch keeps him grounded.

beats.activity: Continue bike mechanic work: wheel truing, cable replacements, inventory reconciliation.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift solid and productive. Rain has mostly cleared by 2pm, but Kwame stays focused on indoor shop tasks. No urge to rush out early; low-key mood from yesterday carries over.

beats.activity: Drive home via I-90 west; SR-520 closure now lifted but traffic heavy.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute; SR-520 reopened after noon, but 6pm traffic is still snarled. Kwame takes I-90 again for consistency. Drizzle returns; he's ready to be home.

beats.activity: Stop at Ballard Market for brewing supplies and fresh produce.
beats.reasoning: Quick neighborhood errand on the way home; Kwame picks up grain and hops for tonight's brewing session. Stays local and efficient in the drizzle.

beats.activity: Home brewing: steep grains, prepare mash, tend to fermentation.; Dinner, review brewing log notes, work on poetry drafts for upcoming open mic.
beats.reasoning: Ritual continuation from yesterday; brewing is meditative and solitary. Kwame's introverted energy fits the quiet evening at home. No poetry open-mic scheduled tonight.
Seven-and-a-half hours of solid sleep to recover from the commute disruption and rainy day. Beats end by 06:00 as required.

diary:
Woke to that grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you grateful for a hot pour-over. The SR-520 closure had everyone talking at the shop; I took I-90 instead and made good time. Spent the morning fixing flats and checking derailleurs, lots of rain damage coming through. The bikes tell you stories if you listen.

Grabbed a sandwich at the Redmond café around noon, just needed to get out of my head for a bit. Afternoon was steady—wheel truing, cable work, inventory. Nothing flashy, but it's honest work.

Drove home through the tail end of the mess, stopped by Ballard Market for some brewing supplies. Got home and lost myself in the grains and fermentation—there's something meditative about it, the same focus I bring to a derailleur adjustment. Worked on some new poetry too, rough stuff, but it's flowing today. Feeling settled.
thought_process: The rain and traffic closure made the day feel compressed, urgent, so I leaned into routine—mechanics at work, brewing at home. Both require precision and patience, which grounded me. The lunch break was necessary; I was getting too wound up thinking about all those frustrated commuters. By evening, the brewing and poetry felt connected somehow—both are about transformation, about waiting for something to develop properly. The drizzle outside my window while I wrote felt less dreary and more like permission to be introspective. My mood shifted from morning tension to evening calm.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
